description_long: This object is a large, horizontally oriented traffic light assembly mounted on a long metal pole. It features three distinct traffic signal units, each with three vertically stacked lights, likely representing the standard red, yellow, and green signals. The pole is robust and metallic, extending horizontally to support the signal units above a roadway, with a vertical support pole at one end for stability. The signal lights are encased in protective housings, and the lenses are clearly visible, with the red lights illuminated. The structure is designed for overhead installation, ensuring visibility to multiple lanes of traffic. The overall finish is industrial, with a metallic gray color dominating the pole and black or dark gray casings for the lights.
description: A large, horizontally mounted traffic light assembly with three signal units on a long metal pole, designed for overhead road installation. The lights are encased in dark housings, with red signals illuminated.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the horizontal pole and all three traffic signal units aligned along it.
description_view_1: End-on view showing the narrow profile of the pole and the stacked arrangement of the signal lights.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the three traffic signal units with their red lights illuminated, and the full horizontal span of the pole.
description_view_3: Another end-on view, similar to view 1, highlighting the vertical stacking of the lights and the slimness of the pole.
